steps.title: Open the judge start guide
steps.proves: The submission has a judge-first entry point with live demo, video, scoring brief, and local proof commands.
steps.expected: A compact start-here file that points to the live demo, GitHub repo, evidence pack, and DataHub artifacts.

steps.title: Run the one-command proof
steps.proves: The core evidence chain can be regenerated locally from source.
steps.expected: A reproducible receipt with generated DataHub, policy, validation, and safety reports.

steps.title: Check DataHub readiness without posting
steps.proves: The optional local DataHub path is explicit, guarded, and not required for judging.
steps.expected: A readiness report that confirms dry-run artifacts are ready and no metadata is posted.

steps.title: Smoke-test the MCP adapter boundary
steps.proves: The local adapter reads DataHub/CAT context before writing bounded receipts.
steps.expected: Three request flows, twelve tool calls, and no external side effects.

steps.title: Run the full local CI equivalent
steps.proves: A fresh-install check, all generators, build, and render/evidence tests pass together.
steps.expected: The build succeeds and the Node test suite reports all tests passing.

safety_summary: No command in the five-minute path posts to DataHub, contacts customers, sends outreach, or requires secrets.
The optional live DataHub mutation remains isolated behind DATAHUB_GMS_URL plus --post.
The MCP smoke test writes only local receipts and records external_side_effects as none.
Missing owner/contact context still produces approval-required or blocked outcomes.
